_comment: Role-default context paths keyed on cohort. Used by context-inject.sh static path. Each entry is an array of {tier, path, rationale} objects. Paths are relative to the project root (or .aihaus/ base). The token '<story-file>' is resolved at runtime by context-inject.sh.

:planner-binding.rationale: Stack, conventions, and milestone history inform planning scope.
Stage gates and source-sync rules shape task planning.
Intent routing decides workflow entry or no-workflow handling.
Business rules are the decision contract for scope and behavior.
Runtime, CI/CD, credential locations, and validation commands are project-specific.
Repository-scoped user preferences shape planning and handoff style.
Analyst brief is the primary input to binding-tier planners.

:planner.rationale: Stack and conventions provide grounding for research and analysis.
Stage gates and workflow protocols define how planned work moves.
Intent routing decides workflow entry or no-workflow handling.
Business rules prevent plans from inventing product behavior.
Environment and CI/CD facts prevent generic validation plans.
Repository-scoped user preferences shape planning and handoff style.
Prior analysis brief gives context on what has already been investigated.

:doer.rationale: Stack, conventions, and verification commands required for implementation.
Stage gates define required handoff and validation evidence.
Intent routing decides whether work continues, starts a workflow, or stays inline.
Runtime, test credentials, deploy jobs, and smoke commands are required execution context.
Repository-scoped user preferences shape implementation and reporting style.
Story spec and acceptance criteria define the implementation target.

:verifier.rationale: Stack, commands, and conventions are required verification context.
Workflow gates define pass, skip, block, and evidence semantics.
Routing context explains why verification belongs to a workflow or inline path.
Business rules define the behavioral contract being verified.
Verification depends on dev URLs, CI jobs, credentials, and smoke commands.
Repository-scoped user preferences shape verification reporting.
Acceptance criteria are the ground truth for verification verdicts.
Execution artifacts and prior summaries provide evidence for verification.

:adversarial-scout.rationale: Stack and conventions expose invalid assumptions in plans.
Workflow gates reveal missing planning and validation obligations.
Routing context exposes invalid workflow entry assumptions.
Business rules are the main source for product-contract contradictions.
Environment facts often expose unvalidated assumptions.
Repository-scoped user preferences can make a plan unacceptable.
Story spec is the scope boundary for adversarial plan review.

:adversarial-review.rationale: Stack and conventions are required review context.
Review must enforce workflow evidence and handoff gates.
Routing context explains intended workflow ownership.
Business rules anchor behavior-affecting review findings.
Review and Playwright checks depend on runtime and credential context.
Repository-scoped user preferences shape review tone and acceptance.
Prior review findings inform adversarial review patterns.
